1|# How the Rork Proxy Works
     2|
     3|The Rork Toolkit server (`EXPO_PUBLIC_TOOLKIT_URL`, e.g. `toolkit.rork.com`) acts as a **reverse proxy** that forwards AI requests to upstream providers. It handles authentication, billing, rate limiting, and cost tracking so that the generated app only needs a single API key.
     4|
     5|## URL structure
     6|
     7|```
     8|${TOOLKIT_URL}/v2/<service>/<upstream-path>
     9|```
    10|
    11|The proxy strips the `/v2/<service>` prefix and forwards the **rest of the path as-is** to the upstream.
    12|
    13|- `/v2/vercel/` → Vercel AI Gateway (`ai-gateway.vercel.sh`). Example: `/v2/vercel/v1/chat/completions` → `ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1/chat/completions`. Gateway speech uses `/v2/vercel/v4/ai/speech-model`; Gateway transcription uses `/v2/vercel/v4/ai/transcription-model`.
    14|- `/v2/elevenlabs/` → ElevenLabs. Example: `/v2/elevenlabs/v1/text-to-speech/...` or `/v2/elevenlabs/v1/speech-to-text`.
    15|- `/v2/exa/` → Exa (`api.exa.ai`). Example: `/v2/exa/search` → `api.exa.ai/search`.
    16|
    17|**The upstream path must be complete and correct**, including version prefixes like `/v1/`, `/v3/ai/`, or `/v4/ai/`. Omitting them (e.g. `/v2/vercel/chat/completions` instead of `/v2/vercel/v1/chat/completions`) will result in a **404** from the upstream.
    18|
    19|## Authentication
    20|
    21|All requests MUST include:
    22|
    23|```
    24|Authorization: Bearer <EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_TOOLKIT_SECRET_KEY>
    25|Content-Type: application/json
    26|```
    27|
    28|The API key is provisioned by calling the `getOrCreateToolkitSecret` tool with a short description of why the app needs Rork AI Cloud. The tool checks Cloud Credits enablement status without exposing the secret value. If Rork AI Cloud is already enabled, continue without asking the user to claim credits. Only ask the user to enable/claim Cloud Credits when the tool returns confirmation required.
    29|
